solutions.solution: (a) Let us check all the options one by one for the required relationship. From option (a), J + P % H × T % L = selected. [Family tree diagram showing Son, Brother, Sister, Mother relationships] J is the brother of T. Clearly, from option (a) it is established that J is the brother of T. So, there is no need to check other options.

(b) Let us check all the options one by one for the required relationship. From option (a), L % R $ D + T × M = Rejected. [Family tree diagram showing Mother, Married couple, Father, Sister relationships] As gender of M is not Known, so M can either be daughter or son of D. From option (b), L + R $ D + M × T = Selected. [Family tree diagram] It is clear from option (b) that M is the daughter of D. So, there is no need to check other options.
(b) I + T % J × L + K = K is the son-in-law of I. [Family tree diagram showing Father, Married couple, Mother, Sister relationships] Clearly, K is the son-in-law of I.

(d) Let us check all the options one by one for the required relationship. From option (a), W % L × T × Y + X = Rejected. [Multiple family tree diagrams for each option] Option (a) is rejected, as it is given that Y cannot be the son of X. From option (b), W + L × T × Y + X = Rejected. Option (b) is rejected, as it is given that Y cannot be the son of X. From option (c), X + L × T × Y + W = Rejected. Option (c) is rejected, as it is given that Y cannot be the son of X. From option (d), W $ X + L + Y + T = Selected. [Final family tree diagram] Y is the grandson of X. Option (d) is selected as it holds that Y is not the son of X but he is the grandson of X. Hence, there is no need to check other options.

(c) Let us check all the options one by one for the required relationship. From option (a), R % T × P + Q + V = Rejected. [Family tree diagram showing Mother, Sister, aunt relationships] T is the aunt of Q.
